The classification of Piptatherum mexicanum provides insight into its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.

intro: Piptatherum mexicanum, also known as Mexican Piptatherum or Piptatherum grass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health issues, and its health benefits include its potential to lower blood pressure, alleviate respiratory problems, and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Piptatherum mexicanum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
